title: How Gnutella Scaled to Handle Query Traffic
excerpt: How Gnutella Scaled to Handle Query Traffic
content_html:
(This is part two of my Gnutella exploration series. Please read the Gnutella Explanation article first if you are not already familiar with Gnutella.)

At its peak, Limewire was installed on a third of desktop computers and estimates from the year prior showed a concurrent node count in the millions. It hit this level of adoption while staying decentralized and avoiding the need for a global file index or coordination servers. More importantly, the network still survives today despite minimal upkeep.

Early versions of Gnutella searched the network using a technique called flood routing. Your computer sent a query to its neighbors. Those neighbors sent it to their neighbors. The process continued until the query ran out of hops or reached a machine with a matching file.

In the previous article, I described Gnutella queries as though they simply flooded across the network. This was true, but I was kinda lying to keep the article short.

Flood routing was OK when Gnutella was an experimental network with a few thousand users. Blindly bothering every reachable computer was no longer viable once the network hit mainstream adoption. Based on my Internet Archive archaeology digs, this practice likely came to an end around the year 2003.

Later Gnutella clients (and the ones surviving today) still pass queries through a mesh of peers, but they are much more selective about which peers they decide to bother. They do this without central file indexes and without requiring any participant to reveal an exact list of shared files.

The newer search system was called the Query Routing Protocol, or QRP.

QRP allowed one computer to advertise a compact, approximate summary of the search terms available behind it. A peer could then ask a useful question before forwarding a query: Is there any chance this connection leads to a matching file? The answer is one of two choices: maybe or certainly not.

This system was more complicated than the flood routing system in the first generation of Gnutella clients, but it scaled nicely.

The Old Routing System

You can't really call it a network protocol if there is only one working implementation. That's just network software, not a protocol. A good protocol should be simple enough that independent developers can implement it, and flood routing met this goal despite efficiency issues.

You can fit the entire query model in your head:

  1. Send a query to every connected peer.
  2. Decrease its TTL.
  3. Have those peers repeat the process until the TTL reaches zero.
  4. Suppress duplicate queries using the query's unique identifier.
  5. Return any results along remembered reverse routes.

This simplicity gave early Gnutella an ecosystem of diverse clients and, importantly, people actually using the network. Flood routing was not free, though.

Suppose every peer has four connections. The first peer might forward a query to four neighbors. Each of those peers normally excludes the connection from which the query arrived, so a tree-like expansion would start at 4, then expand to 12, 36, 108, and so on.

A real mesh contains cycles, duplicate suppression, and peers with different connection counts, so the actual numbers vary. The important part is that the amount of traffic still grows very quickly.

The network therefore spends most of its query bandwidth proving that peers do not have the requested file.

The scalability problems were eventually demonstrated with sciencey PDFs that clearly did the math.

Leaves and Ultrapeers

Early Gnutella was an undifferentiated mesh of peers. Later versions that used QRP divided nodes into two roles:

  • A leaf sat at the edge of the network.
  • An ultrapeer participated in a highly connected upper tier.

This may sound like client-server architecture, but there was no permanent server and no central authority deciding who could become an ultrapeer.

An ultrapeer was still an ordinary Gnutella user running an ordinary Gnutella client. The difference was that an ultrapeer, unlike a leaf node, had enough bandwidth, uptime, memory, and network reach to perform additional routing work. Ultrapeers were usually those super lucky power users with a DSL connection and a high-end Windows 2000 box with 2 GBs of RAM.

Some clients will automatically promote a node based on things such as uptime, firewall status, available memory, file descriptors, and bandwidth.

A more plebeian peer on a dial-up connection behind a firewall, or with low uptime would operate as a leaf.

Leaves maintained connections to several ultrapeers for redundancy. Ultrapeers maintained connections to other ultrapeers and accepted collections of leaves beneath them.

Why Two Tiers?

Consider what happens when a leaf sends a query to its ultrapeer.

Under pure flood routing, the ultrapeer would send that query to every other connected leaf, whether or not any of them shared relevant files.

Under QRP, each leaf periodically gives the ultrapeer a compact representation of all the words in its current file library. It's a bit more complicated than just uploading a list of file names. This is called a QRP table, and the ultrapeer holds onto one QRP table per connected leaf.

When a query arrives, the ultrapeer checks those tables. For example, if a query arrives for Ubuntu Server 2026 Live CD ISO, the ultrapeer might determine that leaves E and C definitely do not have the file, but leaves B and D might. Therefore, the query is sent to B and D but not C or E.

Using an opaque data structure- a Bloom filter, sort of- the yes/no question is much cheaper to answer than wasting bandwidth by sending the full query to every leaf and waiting for most of them to say nothing.

QRP tables are usually used only for leaf-to-ultrapeer routing. They do not generally create a map of the full network. Some nodes support a thing called last-hop QRP, where the ultrapeer creates a merged summary of all connected leaves to improve ultra-to-ultra query routing, but that is a more complicated matter.

False Positives vs. False Negatives

QRP tables are approximate.

A false positive occurs when the table says a peer connection might match, but no matching file exists. The query travels down one unnecessary connection. Some bandwidth is wasted, but the search still works.

A false negative occurs when the table says a connection cannot match even though a real result exists behind it. The query is discarded, and the user never sees the file.

QRP's table construction is therefore conservative. Its not trying to prove that a connection will produce a result. It makes a best effort guess to route queries to useful endpoints.

Let's take a look at how the tables actually work.

Prerequisite Knowledge: Hash Functions

You can skip this section if you already know what a hash function is.

A hash function accepts some input and deterministically converts it into a number.

Imagine a fictional hash function that produces a number from 0 through 999:

hash("ubuntu") = 412
hash("server") = 731
hash("iso")    = 044

The same input always produces the same output.

Different inputs will usually produce different outputs, but not always. There are more possible words than there are numbers from 0 through 999, so eventually two unrelated words must produce the same number. This is called a collision.

QRP uses this property to turn words into table positions.

The hash does not need to be cryptographically secure. We are not protecting a password or verifying a software download. We only need every compatible Gnutella client to calculate the same table position for the same word.

Prerequisite Knowledge: Bloom Filters

You can also skip this section if you already understand Bloom filters.

A Bloom filter is an extremely compact, approximate representation of a set. Its main operation is asking: Is this item in the set?

A Bloom filter can answer in two ways: Probably and Definitely not.

It cannot reliably answer with 100% certainty because unrelated values may collide and set the same positions.

A QRP table feels very much like a Bloom filter, although it is not exactly the textbook data structure.

The useful mental model is still the same: A QRP table is a lossy, compacted set of search terms.

If a required slot is absent, the connection cannot match that term. If the slot is present, the connection is worth considering.

What Gets Stored in a QRP Table?

A client starts by canonicalizing the names of its shared files into tokens. This includes operations such as normalizing text- spaces, capitalization, and so on- and separating it into searchable words.

A file named:

Ubuntu Server 2024.iso

might contribute terms resembling:

ubuntu
server
2024
iso

The client does not necessarily insert only complete words.

For example, a long word might contribute forms conceptually resembling:

ubuntu
ubunt
ubun

This allows some prefix searches to find the correct route without inserting every imaginable substring.

Each indexed form is hashed into a table position:

ubuntu  → hash → slot 41,292
server  → hash → slot 17,104
iso     → hash → slot 52,881

The original text is not stored in that slot. The slot only answers the yes/no question, so slots act more like a checklist.

Repeated words are deduplicated during query matching, and terms shorter than three bytes are excluded from the QRP word vector.

I ran an experiment using a real directory containing approximately 6,300 files with filenames of varying lengths. Storing the filenames as plain text required roughly 365 KiB. A QRP table can summarize those filenames in a fraction of that space.

When compacted to one bit per slot, a 65,536-slot table occupies exactly 8 KiB.

Table sizes are selected in powers of two.

A table that is too small becomes crowded. As more terms collide with one another, more positions are marked as present. Eventually, almost every query appears to match, and the table stops saving bandwidth. Peers can respond by constructing a larger table.

How QRP Tables Move Around

A leaf must send its table to each ultrapeer it connects to.

It would be wasteful to resend a completely unrelated structure every time one file is added or removed, so QRP defines two important message types: RESET and PATCH.

RESET messages instruct a peer to throw away the table currently associated with the sender and prepare a new empty table of a certain size.

PATCH messages then transform that empty table into the sender's current state.

Unchanged entries are represented as zeros or other no-op values. Because most entries usually do not change, those long runs of zeros compress extremely well over the network.

RESET and PATCH messages are direct-connection messages. They are not flooded across the Gnutella network.

A patch sequence may be split across several messages, but the receiver expects the completed sequence to account for every slot in the logical table.

Matching a Query Against a Table

When a search arrives, the receiving client performs approximately the same canonicalization process used while constructing the table.

A query such as:

Ubuntu Server ISO

becomes a vector of QRP-indexed words.

Each indexed term is hashed into a table slot. The client checks whether those slots are present in the table associated with a connection.

The obvious algorithm would require every word to appear:

ubuntu = present
server = present
iso    = present

If even one word were absent, the query would not be sent down that connection. Most clients apply fuzzy matching that sends the query if a certain threshold is met.

Extra Functionality: Ultra-to-Ultra Queries

I mentioned that QRP tables are used for leaf-to-ultrapeer communication. Sometimes QRP tables can be used for ultra-to-ultra query routing.

An ultrapeer can construct a combined table from its own shared files and the QRP tables supplied by eligible, directly connected leaves.

The ultrapeer may send this combined table to neighboring ultrapeers that advertise support for last-hop QRP.

The combined table indicates that the ultrapeer's own files or one of its directly connected leaves might match these terms. It is important to keep in mind that this does not try to map resources beyond the ultrapeer's directly connected leaves.

Suppose an ultrapeer is considering sending a query to a neighboring ultrapeer.

If the query has more than one hop remaining, the ultrapeer does not use the neighbor's QRP table as a hard filter. The neighbor might forward the query farther into the network, beyond the files represented by its local table.

If the query has exactly one hop remaining, the situation is different.

The neighboring ultrapeer cannot send the query farther through the backbone. All it can do is inspect its own files and forward the query to its leaves.

Those are exactly the resources represented by its combined QRP table.

At that point, a negative QRP match becomes useful, because it is confirmable that nothing available on the final hop can match, so there is no need to send the query there.

Conclusion

QRP is a fun but overlooked aspect of late-era Gnutella clients that survived into what is left of the network today. It managed to filter noise effectively at scale without needing the exactness required for a distributed hash table. I am not aware of any extant P2P systems that use QRP tables for content discovery other than Gnutella2.

Not Covered in this Article

There are several implementation details that deserve their own investigation:

  • Four-bit and one-bit PATCH negotiation
  • Patch compression
  • Unicode canonicalization
  • SHA-1 and other URN searches
  • Handling tables that become excessively dense
  • Hops-flow restrictions
  • Flow-control behavior for overloaded leaves
  • How frequently tables are rebuilt and transmitted
  • Compatibility differences between clients
  • GUESS, which provides another way to perform targeted Gnutella searches

Further Reading

  • Gnutella Explanation - Part one of this series
  • Query Routing for the Gnutella Network / mirror - Christopher Rohrs's original QRP proposal
  • GTK-Gnutella source code - One of the best surviving records of deployed Gnutella behavior. QRP implementation shown here
  • Why Gnutella Can't Scale. No, Really - A critique of the original flood-routing network

sentences.en: A peer could then ask a useful question before forwarding a query: Is there any chance this connection leads to a matching file?
sentences.zh: 然后，对等方可以在转发查询之前提出一个有用的问题：此连接是否有可能导致匹配的文件？

sentences.en: The answer is one of two choices: maybe or certainly not.
sentences.zh: 答案是两个选择之一：也许或肯定不是。

blocks.html_en: This system was more complicated than the flood routing system in the first generation of Gnutella clients, but it scaled nicely.

sentences.en: This system was more complicated than the flood routing system in the first generation of Gnutella clients, but it scaled nicely.
sentences.zh: 该系统比第一代 Gnutella 客户端中的泛洪路由系统更复杂，但可扩展性很好。

blocks.html_en: The Old Routing System

sentences.en: The Old Routing System
sentences.zh: 旧的路由系统

blocks.html_en: You can't really call it a network protocol if there is only one working implementation. That's just network software, not a protocol. A good protocol should be simple enough that independent developers can implement it, and flood routing met this goal despite efficiency issues.

sentences.en: You can't really call it a network protocol if there is only one working implementation.
sentences.zh: 如果只有一种有效的实现，你就不能真正将其称为网络协议。

sentences.en: That's just network software, not a protocol.
sentences.zh: 这只是网络软件，而不是协议。

sentences.en: A good protocol should be simple enough that independent developers can implement it, and flood routing met this goal despite efficiency issues.
sentences.zh: 一个好的协议应该足够简单，以便独立开发人员可以实现它，尽管存在效率问题，泛洪路由还是实现了这一目标。

blocks.html_en: You can fit the entire query model in your head:

sentences.en: You can fit the entire query model in your head:
sentences.zh: 您可以将整个查询模型放在您的脑海中：

blocks.html_en: • Send a query to every connected peer.

sentences.en: Send a query to every connected peer.
sentences.zh: 向每个连接的对等点发送查询。

blocks.html_en: • Decrease its TTL.

sentences.en: Decrease its TTL.
sentences.zh: 降低其 TTL。

blocks.html_en: • Have those peers repeat the process until the TTL reaches zero.

sentences.en: Have those peers repeat the process until the TTL reaches zero.
sentences.zh: 让这些对等点重复此过程，直到 TTL 达到零。

blocks.html_en: • Suppress duplicate queries using the query's unique identifier.

sentences.en: Suppress duplicate queries using the query's unique identifier.
sentences.zh: 使用查询的唯一标识符抑制重复查询。

blocks.html_en: • Return any results along remembered reverse routes.

sentences.en: Return any results along remembered reverse routes.
sentences.zh: 返回沿记住的反向路线的任何结果。

blocks.html_en: This simplicity gave early Gnutella an ecosystem of diverse clients and, importantly, people actually using the network. Flood routing was not free, though.

sentences.en: This simplicity gave early Gnutella an ecosystem of diverse clients and, importantly, people actually using the network.
sentences.zh: 这种简单性为早期的 Gnutella 提供了一个由不同客户组成的生态系统，更重要的是，还有实际使用该网络的人们。

sentences.en: Flood routing was not free, though.
sentences.zh: 不过，洪水路由并不是免费的。

blocks.html_en: Suppose every peer has four connections. The first peer might forward a query to four neighbors. Each of those peers normally excludes the connection from which the query arrived, so a tree-like expansion would start at 4, then expand to 12, 36, 108, and so on.

sentences.en: Suppose every peer has four connections.
sentences.zh: 假设每个对等点都有四个连接。

sentences.en: The first peer might forward a query to four neighbors.
sentences.zh: 第一个对等点可能会将查询转发给四个邻居。

sentences.en: Each of those peers normally excludes the connection from which the query arrived, so a tree-like expansion would start at 4, then expand to 12, 36, 108, and so on.
sentences.zh: 这些对等点中的每一个通常都会排除查询到达的连接，因此树状扩展将从 4 开始，然后扩展到 12、36、108 等。

blocks.html_en: A real mesh contains cycles, duplicate suppression, and peers with different connection counts, so the actual numbers vary. The important part is that the amount of traffic still grows very quickly.

sentences.en: A real mesh contains cycles, duplicate suppression, and peers with different connection counts, so the actual numbers vary.
sentences.zh: 真实的网格包含循环、重复抑制和具有不同连接数的对等点，因此实际数量会有所不同。

sentences.en: The important part is that the amount of traffic still grows very quickly.
sentences.zh: 重要的是，流量仍然增长得非常快。

blocks.html_en: The network therefore spends most of its query bandwidth proving that peers do not have the requested file.

sentences.en: The network therefore spends most of its query bandwidth proving that peers do not have the requested file.
sentences.zh: 因此，网络花费大部分查询带宽来证明对等点没有所请求的文件。

blocks.html_en: The scalability problems were eventually demonstrated with sciencey PDFs that clearly did the math.

sentences.en: The scalability problems were eventually demonstrated with sciencey PDFs that clearly did the math.
sentences.zh: 可扩展性问题最终通过科学的 PDF 得到了证明，它清楚地进行了数学计算。

blocks.html_en: Leaves and Ultrapeers

sentences.en: Leaves and Ultrapeers
sentences.zh: 叶子和 Ultrapeers

blocks.html_en: Early Gnutella was an undifferentiated mesh of peers. Later versions that used QRP divided nodes into two roles:

sentences.en: Early Gnutella was an undifferentiated mesh of peers.
sentences.zh: 早期的 Gnutella 是一个无差别的同龄人网络。

sentences.en: Later versions that used QRP divided nodes into two roles:
sentences.zh: 使用 QRP 的后续版本将节点分为两种角色：

blocks.html_en: • A leaf sat at the edge of the network.

sentences.en: A leaf sat at the edge of the network.
sentences.zh: 一片叶子位于网络的边缘。

blocks.html_en: • An ultrapeer participated in a highly connected upper tier.

sentences.en: An ultrapeer participated in a highly connected upper tier.
sentences.zh: 超级节点参与了高度互联的上层。

blocks.html_en: This may sound like client-server architecture, but there was no permanent server and no central authority deciding who could become an ultrapeer.

sentences.en: This may sound like client-server architecture, but there was no permanent server and no central authority deciding who could become an ultrapeer.
sentences.zh: 这听起来像是客户端-服务器架构，但没有永久的服务器，也没有中央机构来决定谁可以成为超级节点。

blocks.html_en: An ultrapeer was still an ordinary Gnutella user running an ordinary Gnutella client. The difference was that an ultrapeer, unlike a leaf node, had enough bandwidth, uptime, memory, and network reach to perform additional routing work. Ultrapeers were usually those super lucky power users with a DSL connection and a high-end Windows 2000 box with 2 GBs of RAM.

sentences.en: An ultrapeer was still an ordinary Gnutella user running an ordinary Gnutella client.
sentences.zh: ultrapeer 仍然是运行普通 Gnutella 客户端的普通 Gnutella 用户。

sentences.en: The difference was that an ultrapeer, unlike a leaf node, had enough bandwidth, uptime, memory, and network reach to perform additional routing work.
sentences.zh: 不同之处在于，与叶节点不同，超级对等点具有足够的带宽、正常运行时间、内存和网络范围来执行额外的路由工作。

sentences.en: Ultrapeers were usually those super lucky power users with a DSL connection and a high-end Windows 2000 box with 2 GBs of RAM.
sentences.zh: Ultrapeers 通常是那些超级幸运的高级用户，拥有 DSL 连接和具有 2 GB RAM 的高端 Windows 2000 机器。

blocks.html_en: Some clients will automatically promote a node based on things such as uptime, firewall status, available memory, file descriptors, and bandwidth.

sentences.en: Some clients will automatically promote a node based on things such as uptime, firewall status, available memory, file descriptors, and bandwidth.
sentences.zh: 一些客户端会根据正常运行时间、防火墙状态、可用内存、文件描述符和带宽等因素自动升级节点。

blocks.html_en: A more plebeian peer on a dial-up connection behind a firewall, or with low uptime would operate as a leaf.

sentences.en: A more plebeian peer on a dial-up connection behind a firewall, or with low uptime would operate as a leaf.
sentences.zh: 在防火墙后面的拨号连接上或正常运行时间较短的更平民的对等点将作为叶子运行。

blocks.html_en: Leaves maintained connections to several ultrapeers for redundancy. Ultrapeers maintained connections to other ultrapeers and accepted collections of leaves beneath them.

sentences.en: Leaves maintained connections to several ultrapeers for redundancy.
sentences.zh: 保留与多个超级对等点的维护连接以实现冗余。

sentences.en: Ultrapeers maintained connections to other ultrapeers and accepted collections of leaves beneath them.
sentences.zh: 超级节点与其他超级节点保持联系并接受其下方的叶子集合。

blocks.html_en: Why Two Tiers?

sentences.en: Why Two Tiers?
sentences.zh: 为什么是两层？

blocks.html_en: Consider what happens when a leaf sends a query to its ultrapeer.

sentences.en: Consider what happens when a leaf sends a query to its ultrapeer.
sentences.zh: 考虑当叶子向其超级对等点发送查询时会发生什么。

blocks.html_en: Under pure flood routing, the ultrapeer would send that query to every other connected leaf, whether or not any of them shared relevant files.

sentences.en: Under pure flood routing, the ultrapeer would send that query to every other connected leaf, whether or not any of them shared relevant files.
sentences.zh: 在纯泛洪路由下，ultrapeer 会将该查询发送到每个其他连接的叶子，无论它们中的任何一个是否共享相关文件。

blocks.html_en: Under QRP, each leaf periodically gives the ultrapeer a compact representation of all the words in its current file library. It's a bit more complicated than just uploading a list of file names. This is called a QRP table, and the ultrapeer holds onto one QRP table per connected leaf.

sentences.en: Under QRP, each leaf periodically gives the ultrapeer a compact representation of all the words in its current file library.
sentences.zh: 在 QRP 下，每个叶子定期向 ultrapeer 提供其当前文件库中所有单词的紧凑表示。

sentences.en: It's a bit more complicated than just uploading a list of file names.
sentences.zh: 这比仅仅上传文件名列表要复杂一些。

sentences.en: This is called a QRP table, and the ultrapeer holds onto one QRP table per connected leaf.
sentences.zh: 这称为 QRP 表，ultrapeer 为每个连接的叶子保留一个 QRP 表。

blocks.html_en: When a query arrives, the ultrapeer checks those tables. For example, if a query arrives for Ubuntu Server 2026 Live CD ISO, the ultrapeer might determine that leaves E and C definitely do not have the file, but leaves B and D might. Therefore, the query is sent to B and D but not C or E.

sentences.en: When a query arrives, the ultrapeer checks those tables.
sentences.zh: 当查询到达时，ultrapeer 会检查这些表。

sentences.en: For example, if a query arrives for Ubuntu Server 2026 Live CD ISO, the ultrapeer might determine that leaves E and C definitely do not have the file, but leaves B and D might.
sentences.zh: 例如，如果针对 Ubuntu Server 2026 Live CD ISO 的查询到达，ultrapeer 可能会确定叶子 E 和 C 肯定没有该文件，但叶子 B 和 D 可能有该文件。

sentences.en: Therefore, the query is sent to B and D but not C or E.
sentences.zh: 因此，查询被发送到 B 和 D，而不是 C 或 E。

blocks.html_en: Using an opaque data structure- a Bloom filter, sort of- the yes/no question is much cheaper to answer than wasting bandwidth by sending the full query to every leaf and waiting for most of them to say nothing.

sentences.en: Using an opaque data structure- a Bloom filter, sort of- the yes/no question is much cheaper to answer than wasting bandwidth by sending the full query to every leaf and waiting for most of them to say nothing.
sentences.zh: 使用不透明的数据结构（类似布隆过滤器），回答是/否问题比通过向每个叶子发送完整查询并等待大多数叶子不说话而浪费带宽要便宜得多。

blocks.html_en: QRP tables are usually used only for leaf-to-ultrapeer routing. They do not generally create a map of the full network. Some nodes support a thing called last-hop QRP, where the ultrapeer creates a merged summary of all connected leaves to improve ultra-to-ultra query routing, but that is a more complicated matter.

sentences.en: QRP tables are usually used only for leaf-to-ultrapeer routing.
sentences.zh: QRP 表通常仅用于叶到超级路由。

sentences.en: They do not generally create a map of the full network.
sentences.zh: 他们通常不会创建完整网络的地图。

sentences.en: Some nodes support a thing called last-hop QRP, where the ultrapeer creates a merged summary of all connected leaves to improve ultra-to-ultra query routing, but that is a more complicated matter.
sentences.zh: 一些节点支持称为最后一跳 QRP 的功能，其中 ultrapeer 创建所有连接叶的合并摘要，以改进超到超查询路由，但这是一个更复杂的问题。

blocks.html_en: False Positives vs. False Negatives

sentences.en: False Positives vs. False Negatives
sentences.zh: 误报与漏报

blocks.html_en: QRP tables are approximate.

sentences.en: QRP tables are approximate.
sentences.zh: QRP 表为近似值。

blocks.html_en: A false positive occurs when the table says a peer connection might match, but no matching file exists. The query travels down one unnecessary connection. Some bandwidth is wasted, but the search still works.

sentences.en: A false positive occurs when the table says a peer connection might match, but no matching file exists.
sentences.zh: 当表显示对等连接可能匹配但不存在匹配文件时，就会发生误报。

sentences.en: The query travels down one unnecessary connection.
sentences.zh: 该查询沿着一个不必要的连接进行。

sentences.en: Some bandwidth is wasted, but the search still works.
sentences.zh: 浪费了一些带宽，但搜索仍然有效。

blocks.html_en: A false negative occurs when the table says a connection cannot match even though a real result exists behind it. The query is discarded, and the user never sees the file.

sentences.en: A false negative occurs when the table says a connection cannot match even though a real result exists behind it.
sentences.zh: 当表显示连接无法匹配时，即使其后面存在真实结果，也会出现漏报。

sentences.en: The query is discarded, and the user never sees the file.
sentences.zh: 查询被丢弃，用户永远看不到该文件。

blocks.html_en: QRP's table construction is therefore conservative. Its not trying to prove that a connection will produce a result. It makes a best effort guess to route queries to useful endpoints.

sentences.en: QRP's table construction is therefore conservative.
sentences.zh: 因此，QRP 的表结构是保守的。

sentences.en: Its not trying to prove that a connection will produce a result.
sentences.zh: 它并不是试图证明连接会产生结果。

sentences.en: It makes a best effort guess to route queries to useful endpoints.
sentences.zh: 它尽最大努力猜测将查询路由到有用的端点。

blocks.html_en: Let's take a look at how the tables actually work.

sentences.en: Let's take a look at how the tables actually work.
sentences.zh: 让我们看一下这些表的实际工作原理。

blocks.html_en: Prerequisite Knowledge: Hash Functions

sentences.en: Prerequisite Knowledge: Hash Functions
sentences.zh: 必备知识：哈希函数

blocks.html_en: You can skip this section if you already know what a hash function is.

sentences.en: You can skip this section if you already know what a hash function is.
sentences.zh: 如果您已经知道什么是哈希函数，则可以跳过本节。

blocks.html_en: A hash function accepts some input and deterministically converts it into a number.

sentences.en: A hash function accepts some input and deterministically converts it into a number.
sentences.zh: 哈希函数接受一些输入并确定地将其转换为数字。

blocks.html_en: Imagine a fictional hash function that produces a number from 0 through 999:

sentences.en: Imagine a fictional hash function that produces a number from 0 through 999:
sentences.zh: 想象一个虚构的哈希函数，它生成 0 到 999 之间的数字：

blocks.html_en: The same input always produces the same output.

sentences.en: The same input always produces the same output.
sentences.zh: 相同的输入总是产生相同的输出。

blocks.html_en: Different inputs will usually produce different outputs, but not always. There are more possible words than there are numbers from 0 through 999, so eventually two unrelated words must produce the same number. This is called a collision.

sentences.en: Different inputs will usually produce different outputs, but not always.
sentences.zh: 不同的输入通常会产生不同的输出，但并非总是如此。

sentences.en: There are more possible words than there are numbers from 0 through 999, so eventually two unrelated words must produce the same number.
sentences.zh: 可能的单词数量比 0 到 999 之间的数字还要多，因此最终两个不相关的单词一定会产生相同的数字。

sentences.en: This is called a collision.
sentences.zh: 这称为碰撞。

blocks.html_en: QRP uses this property to turn words into table positions.

sentences.en: QRP uses this property to turn words into table positions.
sentences.zh: QRP 使用此属性将单词转换为表格位置。

blocks.html_en: The hash does not need to be cryptographically secure. We are not protecting a password or verifying a software download. We only need every compatible Gnutella client to calculate the same table position for the same word.

sentences.en: The hash does not need to be cryptographically secure.
sentences.zh: 哈希值不需要加密安全。

sentences.en: We are not protecting a password or verifying a software download.
sentences.zh: 我们不保护密码或验证软件下载。

sentences.en: We only need every compatible Gnutella client to calculate the same table position for the same word.
sentences.zh: 我们只需要每个兼容的 Gnutella 客户端计算相同单词的相同表格位置。

blocks.html_en: Prerequisite Knowledge: Bloom Filters

sentences.en: Prerequisite Knowledge: Bloom Filters
sentences.zh: 必备知识：布隆过滤器

blocks.html_en: You can also skip this section if you already understand Bloom filters.

sentences.en: You can also skip this section if you already understand Bloom filters.
sentences.zh: 如果您已经了解布隆过滤器，您也可以跳过本节。

blocks.html_en: A Bloom filter is an extremely compact, approximate representation of a set. Its main operation is asking: Is this item in the set?

sentences.en: A Bloom filter is an extremely compact, approximate representation of a set.
sentences.zh: 布隆过滤器是一个极其紧凑的集合的近似表示。

sentences.en: Its main operation is asking: Is this item in the set?
sentences.zh: 它的主要操作是询问：这个项目在集合中吗？

blocks.html_en: A Bloom filter can answer in two ways: Probably and Definitely not.

sentences.en: A Bloom filter can answer in two ways: Probably and Definitely not.
sentences.zh: 布隆过滤器可以用两种方式回答：可能和绝对不是。

blocks.html_en: It cannot reliably answer with 100% certainty because unrelated values may collide and set the same positions.

sentences.en: It cannot reliably answer with 100% certainty because unrelated values may collide and set the same positions.
sentences.zh: 它无法 100% 确定地可靠地回答，因为不相关的值可能会发生冲突并设置相同的位置。

blocks.html_en: A QRP table feels very much like a Bloom filter, although it is not exactly the textbook data structure.

sentences.en: A QRP table feels very much like a Bloom filter, although it is not exactly the textbook data structure.
sentences.zh: QRP 表感觉非常像布隆过滤器，尽管它并不完全是教科书的数据结构。

blocks.html_en: The useful mental model is still the same: A QRP table is a lossy, compacted set of search terms.

sentences.en: The useful mental model is still the same: A QRP table is a lossy, compacted set of search terms.
sentences.zh: 有用的心智模型仍然相同：QRP 表是一组有损、紧凑的搜索项。

blocks.html_en: If a required slot is absent, the connection cannot match that term. If the slot is present, the connection is worth considering.

sentences.en: If a required slot is absent, the connection cannot match that term.
sentences.zh: 如果缺少所需的插槽，则连接无法匹配该术语。

sentences.en: If the slot is present, the connection is worth considering.
sentences.zh: 如果存在插槽，则值得考虑该连接。

blocks.html_en: What Gets Stored in a QRP Table?

sentences.en: What Gets Stored in a QRP Table?
sentences.zh: QRP 表中存储什么？

blocks.html_en: A client starts by canonicalizing the names of its shared files into tokens. This includes operations such as normalizing text- spaces, capitalization, and so on- and separating it into searchable words.

sentences.en: A client starts by canonicalizing the names of its shared files into tokens.
sentences.zh: 客户端首先将其共享文件的名称规范化为令牌。

sentences.en: This includes operations such as normalizing text- spaces, capitalization, and so on- and separating it into searchable words.
sentences.zh: 这包括诸如标准化文本空间、大写等以及将其分离为可搜索单词等操作。

blocks.html_en: A file named:

sentences.en: A file named:
sentences.zh: 一个名为：

blocks.html_en: might contribute terms resembling:

sentences.en: might contribute terms resembling:
sentences.zh: 可能会提供类似以下的术语：

blocks.html_en: The client does not necessarily insert only complete words.

sentences.en: The client does not necessarily insert only complete words.
sentences.zh: 客户端不一定只插入完整的单词。

blocks.html_en: For example, a long word might contribute forms conceptually resembling:

sentences.en: For example, a long word might contribute forms conceptually resembling:
sentences.zh: 例如，一个长单词可能会提供概念上类似于以下的形式：

blocks.html_en: This allows some prefix searches to find the correct route without inserting every imaginable substring.

sentences.en: This allows some prefix searches to find the correct route without inserting every imaginable substring.
sentences.zh: 这允许一些前缀搜索找到正确的路线，而无需插入每个可以想象的子字符串。

blocks.html_en: Each indexed form is hashed into a table position:

sentences.en: Each indexed form is hashed into a table position:
sentences.zh: 每个索引形式都被散列到表位置中：

blocks.html_en: The original text is not stored in that slot. The slot only answers the yes/no question, so slots act more like a checklist.

sentences.en: The original text is not stored in that slot.
sentences.zh: 原始文本不存储在该槽中。

sentences.en: The slot only answers the yes/no question, so slots act more like a checklist.
sentences.zh: 插槽仅回答是/否问题，因此插槽更像是一个清单。

blocks.html_en: Repeated words are deduplicated during query matching, and terms shorter than three bytes are excluded from the QRP word vector.

sentences.en: Repeated words are deduplicated during query matching, and terms shorter than three bytes are excluded from the QRP word vector.
sentences.zh: 在查询匹配过程中会删除重复的单词，并且将短于三个字节的术语从 QRP 单词向量中排除。

blocks.html_en: I ran an experiment using a real directory containing approximately 6,300 files with filenames of varying lengths. Storing the filenames as plain text required roughly 365 KiB. A QRP table can summarize those filenames in a fraction of that space.

sentences.en: I ran an experiment using a real directory containing approximately 6,300 files with filenames of varying lengths.
sentences.zh: 我使用一个包含大约 6,300 个文件（文件名长度不同）的真实目录进行了一项实验。

sentences.en: Storing the filenames as plain text required roughly 365 KiB.
sentences.zh: 将文件名存储为纯文本大约需要 365 KiB。

sentences.en: A QRP table can summarize those filenames in a fraction of that space.
sentences.zh: QRP 表可以在该空间的一小部分中汇总这些文件名。

blocks.html_en: When compacted to one bit per slot, a 65,536-slot table occupies exactly 8 KiB.

sentences.en: When compacted to one bit per slot, a 65,536-slot table occupies exactly 8 KiB.
sentences.zh: 当压缩为每槽一位时，65,536 槽表正好占用 8 KiB。

blocks.html_en: Table sizes are selected in powers of two.

sentences.en: Table sizes are selected in powers of two.
sentences.zh: 表格大小以 2 的幂为单位选择。

blocks.html_en: A table that is too small becomes crowded. As more terms collide with one another, more positions are marked as present. Eventually, almost every query appears to match, and the table stops saving bandwidth. Peers can respond by constructing a larger table.

sentences.en: A table that is too small becomes crowded.
sentences.zh: 桌子太小就会变得拥挤。

sentences.en: As more terms collide with one another, more positions are marked as present.
sentences.zh: 随着越来越多的术语相互冲突，更多的位置被标记为存在。

sentences.en: Eventually, almost every query appears to match, and the table stops saving bandwidth.
sentences.zh: 最终，几乎每个查询似乎都匹配，并且该表停止节省带宽。

sentences.en: Peers can respond by constructing a larger table.
sentences.zh: 同行可以通过构建更大的表来响应。

blocks.html_en: How QRP Tables Move Around

sentences.en: How QRP Tables Move Around
sentences.zh: QRP 表如何移动

blocks.html_en: A leaf must send its table to each ultrapeer it connects to.

sentences.en: A leaf must send its table to each ultrapeer it connects to.
sentences.zh: 叶子必须将其表发送到它连接的每个超级对等体。

blocks.html_en: It would be wasteful to resend a completely unrelated structure every time one file is added or removed, so QRP defines two important message types: RESET and PATCH.

sentences.en: It would be wasteful to resend a completely unrelated structure every time one file is added or removed, so QRP defines two important message types: RESET and PATCH.
sentences.zh: 每次添加或删除一个文件时都重新发送完全不相关的结构是很浪费的，因此QRP定义了两种重要的消息类型：RESET和PATCH。

blocks.html_en: RESET messages instruct a peer to throw away the table currently associated with the sender and prepare a new empty table of a certain size.

sentences.en: RESET messages instruct a peer to throw away the table currently associated with the sender and prepare a new empty table of a certain size.
sentences.zh: RESET 消息指示对等方丢弃当前与发送方关联的表并准备一个特定大小的新空表。

blocks.html_en: PATCH messages then transform that empty table into the sender's current state.

sentences.en: PATCH messages then transform that empty table into the sender's current state.
sentences.zh: 然后 PATCH 消息将该空表转换为发送者的当前状态。

blocks.html_en: Unchanged entries are represented as zeros or other no-op values. Because most entries usually do not change, those long runs of zeros compress extremely well over the network.

sentences.en: Unchanged entries are represented as zeros or other no-op values.
sentences.zh: 未更改的条目表示为零或其他无操作值。

sentences.en: Because most entries usually do not change, those long runs of zeros compress extremely well over the network.
sentences.zh: 因为大多数条目通常不会改变，所以那些长串的零在网络上压缩得非常好。

blocks.html_en: RESET and PATCH messages are direct-connection messages. They are not flooded across the Gnutella network.

sentences.en: RESET and PATCH messages are direct-connection messages.
sentences.zh: RESET和PATCH消息是直连消息。

sentences.en: They are not flooded across the Gnutella network.
sentences.zh: 它们不会在 Gnutella 网络中泛滥。

blocks.html_en: A patch sequence may be split across several messages, but the receiver expects the completed sequence to account for every slot in the logical table.

sentences.en: A patch sequence may be split across several messages, but the receiver expects the completed sequence to account for every slot in the logical table.
sentences.zh: 补丁序列可以分为多个消息，但接收者期望完整的序列能够解释逻辑表中的每个槽。

blocks.html_en: When a search arrives, the receiving client performs approximately the same canonicalization process used while constructing the table.

blocks.html_en: becomes a vector of QRP-indexed words.

sentences.en: becomes a vector of QRP-indexed words.

sentences.en: Each indexed term is hashed into a table slot.

sentences.en: The client checks whether those slots are present in the table associated with a connection.

blocks.html_en: The obvious algorithm would require every word to appear:

sentences.zh: 即使缺少一个单词，查询也不会通过该连接发送。

sentences.en: Most clients apply fuzzy matching that sends the query if a certain threshold is met.
sentences.zh: 大多数客户端应用模糊匹配，在满足特定阈值时发送查询。

blocks.html_en: I mentioned that QRP tables are used for leaf-to-ultrapeer communication. Sometimes QRP tables can be used for ultra-to-ultra query routing.

sentences.en: I mentioned that QRP tables are used for leaf-to-ultrapeer communication.
sentences.zh: 我提到过 QRP 表用于 leaf-to-ultrapeer 通信。

sentences.en: Sometimes QRP tables can be used for ultra-to-ultra query routing.
sentences.zh: 有时QRP表可用于超到超查询路由。

blocks.html_en: An ultrapeer can construct a combined table from its own shared files and the QRP tables supplied by eligible, directly connected leaves.

sentences.en: An ultrapeer can construct a combined table from its own shared files and the QRP tables supplied by eligible, directly connected leaves.
sentences.zh: 超级节点可以从其自己的共享文件和合格的直接连接的叶子提供的 QRP 表构建组合表。

blocks.html_en: The ultrapeer may send this combined table to neighboring ultrapeers that advertise support for last-hop QRP.

sentences.en: The ultrapeer may send this combined table to neighboring ultrapeers that advertise support for last-hop QRP.
sentences.zh: 超级对等体可以将该组合表发送到通告对最后一跳QRP的支持的相邻超级对等体。

blocks.html_en: The combined table indicates that the ultrapeer's own files or one of its directly connected leaves might match these terms. It is important to keep in mind that this does not try to map resources beyond the ultrapeer's directly connected leaves.

sentences.en: The combined table indicates that the ultrapeer's own files or one of its directly connected leaves might match these terms.
sentences.zh: 组合表表明超级对等点自己的文件或其直接连接的叶子之一可能与这些术语匹配。

sentences.en: It is important to keep in mind that this does not try to map resources beyond the ultrapeer's directly connected leaves.
sentences.zh: 重要的是要记住，这不会尝试将资源映射到超级对等直接连接的叶子之外。

blocks.html_en: Suppose an ultrapeer is considering sending a query to a neighboring ultrapeer.

sentences.en: Suppose an ultrapeer is considering sending a query to a neighboring ultrapeer.
sentences.zh: 假设一个超级对等体正在考虑向相邻的超级对等体发送查询。

blocks.html_en: If the query has more than one hop remaining, the ultrapeer does not use the neighbor's QRP table as a hard filter. The neighbor might forward the query farther into the network, beyond the files represented by its local table.

sentences.en: If the query has more than one hop remaining, the ultrapeer does not use the neighbor's QRP table as a hard filter.
sentences.zh: 如果查询剩余多于一跳，ultrapeer 不会使用邻居的 QRP 表作为硬过滤器。

sentences.en: The neighbor might forward the query farther into the network, beyond the files represented by its local table.
sentences.zh: 邻居可能会将查询进一步转发到网络中，超出其本地表所表示的文件。

blocks.html_en: If the query has exactly one hop remaining, the situation is different.

sentences.en: If the query has exactly one hop remaining, the situation is different.
sentences.zh: 如果查询仅剩一跳，情况就不同了。

blocks.html_en: The neighboring ultrapeer cannot send the query farther through the backbone. All it can do is inspect its own files and forward the query to its leaves.

sentences.en: The neighboring ultrapeer cannot send the query farther through the backbone.
sentences.zh: 相邻的超级对等点无法通过主干网将查询发送得更远。

sentences.en: All it can do is inspect its own files and forward the query to its leaves.
sentences.zh: 它所能做的就是检查它自己的文件并将查询转发到它的叶子。

blocks.html_en: Those are exactly the resources represented by its combined QRP table.

sentences.en: Those are exactly the resources represented by its combined QRP table.
sentences.zh: 这些正是其组合的 QRP 表所代表的资源。

blocks.html_en: At that point, a negative QRP match becomes useful, because it is confirmable that nothing available on the final hop can match, so there is no need to send the query there.

sentences.en: At that point, a negative QRP match becomes useful, because it is confirmable that nothing available on the final hop can match, so there is no need to send the query there.
sentences.zh: 此时，负的 QRP 匹配就变得有用了，因为可以确认最后一跳上没有任何可用的内容可以匹配，因此不需要在那里发送查询。

blocks.html_en: Conclusion

sentences.en: Conclusion
sentences.zh: 结论

blocks.html_en: QRP is a fun but overlooked aspect of late-era Gnutella clients that survived into what is left of the network today. It managed to filter noise effectively at scale without needing the exactness required for a distributed hash table. I am not aware of any extant P2P systems that use QRP tables for content discovery other than Gnutella2.

sentences.en: QRP is a fun but overlooked aspect of late-era Gnutella clients that survived into what is left of the network today.
sentences.zh: QRP 是晚期 Gnutella 客户端的一个有趣但被忽视的方面，它幸存到了今天的网络中。

sentences.en: It managed to filter noise effectively at scale without needing the exactness required for a distributed hash table.
sentences.zh: 它能够有效地大规模过滤噪声，而无需分布式哈希表所需的精确性。

sentences.zh: 我不知道除了 Gnutella2 之外还有任何现有的 P2P 系统使用 QRP 表进行内容发现。

content_html_zh:
（这是我的 Gnutella 探索系列的第二部分。 如果您还不熟悉 Gnutella，请先阅读 Gnutella 解释文章。）

在鼎盛时期，Limewire 被安装在三分之一的台式计算机上，根据前一年的估计，并发节点数达到数百万。 它达到了这种采用水平，同时保持去中心化并避免了对全局文件索引或协调服务器的需要。 更重要的是，尽管维护很少，但该网络至今仍然存在。

Gnutella 的早期版本使用一种称为泛洪路由的技术来搜索网络。 您的计算机向其邻居发送了查询。 那些邻居将其发送给他们的邻居。 该过程持续进行，直到查询耗尽跳数或到达具有匹配文件的机器。

在上一篇文章中，我将 Gnutella 查询描述为好像它们只是在网络中泛滥一样。 这是真的，但为了让文章简短，我有点撒谎了。

当 Gnutella 是一个拥有数千名用户的实验网络时，泛洪路由是可以接受的。 一旦网络成为主流，盲目地打扰每台可访问的计算机就不再可行。 根据我对互联网档案馆的考古挖掘，这种做法很可能在 2003 年左右结束。

后来的 Gnutella 客户端（以及今天幸存的客户端）仍然通过对等点网络传递查询，但他们对决定打扰哪些对等点更具选择性。 他们在没有中央文件索引的情况下做到这一点，并且不需要任何参与者透露共享文件的确切列表。

较新的搜索系统称为查询路由协议或 QRP。

QRP 允许一台计算机发布其背后可用搜索词的紧凑、近似摘要。 然后，对等方可以在转发查询之前提出一个有用的问题：此连接是否有可能导致匹配的文件？ 答案是两个选择之一：也许或肯定不是。

该系统比第一代 Gnutella 客户端中的泛洪路由系统更复杂，但可扩展性很好。

旧的路由系统

如果只有一种有效的实现，你就不能真正将其称为网络协议。 这只是网络软件，而不是协议。 一个好的协议应该足够简单，以便独立开发人员可以实现它，尽管存在效率问题，泛洪路由还是实现了这一目标。

您可以将整个查询模型放在您的脑海中：

  1. 向每个连接的对等点发送查询。
  2. 降低其 TTL。
  3. 让这些对等点重复此过程，直到 TTL 达到零。
  4. 使用查询的唯一标识符抑制重复查询。
  5. 返回沿记住的反向路线的任何结果。

这种简单性为早期的 Gnutella 提供了一个由不同客户组成的生态系统，更重要的是，还有实际使用该网络的人们。 不过，洪水路由并不是免费的。

假设每个对等点都有四个连接。 第一个对等点可能会将查询转发给四个邻居。 这些对等点中的每一个通常都会排除查询到达的连接，因此树状扩展将从 4 开始，然后扩展到 12、36、108 等。

真实的网格包含循环、重复抑制和具有不同连接数的对等点，因此实际数量会有所不同。 重要的是，流量仍然增长得非常快。

因此，网络花费大部分查询带宽来证明对等点没有所请求的文件。

可扩展性问题最终通过科学的 PDF 得到了证明，它清楚地进行了数学计算。

叶子和 Ultrapeers

早期的 Gnutella 是一个无差别的同龄人网络。 使用 QRP 的后续版本将节点分为两种角色：

  • 一片叶子位于网络的边缘。
  • 超级节点参与了高度互联的上层。

这听起来像是客户端-服务器架构，但没有永久的服务器，也没有中央机构来决定谁可以成为超级节点。

ultrapeer 仍然是运行普通 Gnutella 客户端的普通 Gnutella 用户。 不同之处在于，与叶节点不同，超级对等点具有足够的带宽、正常运行时间、内存和网络范围来执行额外的路由工作。 Ultrapeers 通常是那些超级幸运的高级用户，拥有 DSL 连接和具有 2 GB RAM 的高端 Windows 2000 机器。

一些客户端会根据正常运行时间、防火墙状态、可用内存、文件描述符和带宽等因素自动升级节点。

在防火墙后面的拨号连接上或正常运行时间较短的更平民的对等点将作为叶子运行。

保留与多个超级对等点的维护连接以实现冗余。 超级节点与其他超级节点保持联系并接受其下方的叶子集合。

为什么是两层？

考虑当叶子向其超级对等点发送查询时会发生什么。

在纯泛洪路由下，ultrapeer 会将该查询发送到每个其他连接的叶子，无论它们中的任何一个是否共享相关文件。

在 QRP 下，每个叶子定期向 ultrapeer 提供其当前文件库中所有单词的紧凑表示。 这比仅仅上传文件名列表要复杂一些。 这称为 QRP 表，ultrapeer 为每个连接的叶子保留一个 QRP 表。

当查询到达时，ultrapeer 会检查这些表。 例如，如果针对 Ubuntu Server 2026 Live CD ISO 的查询到达，ultrapeer 可能会确定叶子 E 和 C 肯定没有该文件，但叶子 B 和 D 可能有该文件。 因此，查询被发送到 B 和 D，而不是 C 或 E。

使用不透明的数据结构（类似布隆过滤器），回答是/否问题比通过向每个叶子发送完整查询并等待大多数叶子不说话而浪费带宽要便宜得多。

QRP 表通常仅用于叶到超级路由。 他们通常不会创建完整网络的地图。 一些节点支持称为最后一跳 QRP 的功能，其中 ultrapeer 创建所有连接叶的合并摘要，以改进超到超查询路由，但这是一个更复杂的问题。

误报与漏报

QRP 表为近似值。

当表显示对等连接可能匹配但不存在匹配文件时，就会发生误报。 该查询沿着一个不必要的连接进行。 浪费了一些带宽，但搜索仍然有效。

当表显示连接无法匹配时，即使其后面存在真实结果，也会出现漏报。 查询被丢弃，用户永远看不到该文件。

因此，QRP 的表结构是保守的。 它并不是试图证明连接会产生结果。 它尽最大努力猜测将查询路由到有用的端点。

让我们看一下这些表的实际工作原理。

必备知识：哈希函数

如果您已经知道什么是哈希函数，则可以跳过本节。

哈希函数接受一些输入并确定地将其转换为数字。

想象一个虚构的哈希函数，它生成 0 到 999 之间的数字：

hash("ubuntu") = 412
hash("server") = 731
hash("iso")    = 044

相同的输入总是产生相同的输出。

不同的输入通常会产生不同的输出，但并非总是如此。 可能的单词数量比 0 到 999 之间的数字还要多，因此最终两个不相关的单词一定会产生相同的数字。 这称为碰撞。

QRP 使用此属性将单词转换为表格位置。

哈希值不需要加密安全。 我们不保护密码或验证软件下载。 我们只需要每个兼容的 Gnutella 客户端计算相同单词的相同表格位置。

必备知识：布隆过滤器

如果您已经了解布隆过滤器，您也可以跳过本节。

布隆过滤器是一个极其紧凑的集合的近似表示。 它的主要操作是询问：这个项目在集合中吗？

布隆过滤器可以用两种方式回答：可能和绝对不是。

它无法 100% 确定地可靠地回答，因为不相关的值可能会发生冲突并设置相同的位置。

QRP 表感觉非常像布隆过滤器，尽管它并不完全是教科书的数据结构。

有用的心智模型仍然相同：QRP 表是一组有损、紧凑的搜索项。

如果缺少所需的插槽，则连接无法匹配该术语。 如果存在插槽，则值得考虑该连接。

QRP 表中存储什么？

客户端首先将其共享文件的名称规范化为令牌。 这包括诸如标准化文本空间、大写等以及将其分离为可搜索单词等操作。

一个名为：

Ubuntu Server 2024.iso

可能会提供类似以下的术语：

ubuntu
server
2024
iso

客户端不一定只插入完整的单词。

例如，一个长单词可能会提供概念上类似于以下的形式：

ubuntu
ubunt
ubun

这允许一些前缀搜索找到正确的路线，而无需插入每个可以想象的子字符串。

每个索引形式都被散列到表位置中：

ubuntu  → hash → slot 41,292
server  → hash → slot 17,104
iso     → hash → slot 52,881

原始文本不存储在该槽中。 插槽仅回答是/否问题，因此插槽更像是一个清单。

在查询匹配过程中会删除重复的单词，并且将短于三个字节的术语从 QRP 单词向量中排除。

我使用一个包含大约 6,300 个文件（文件名长度不同）的真实目录进行了一项实验。 将文件名存储为纯文本大约需要 365 KiB。 QRP 表可以在该空间的一小部分中汇总这些文件名。

当压缩为每槽一位时，65,536 槽表正好占用 8 KiB。

表格大小以 2 的幂为单位选择。

桌子太小就会变得拥挤。 随着越来越多的术语相互冲突，更多的位置被标记为存在。 最终，几乎每个查询似乎都匹配，并且该表停止节省带宽。 同行可以通过构建更大的表来响应。

QRP 表如何移动

叶子必须将其表发送到它连接的每个超级对等体。

每次添加或删除一个文件时都重新发送完全不相关的结构是很浪费的，因此QRP定义了两种重要的消息类型：RESET和PATCH。

RESET 消息指示对等方丢弃当前与发送方关联的表并准备一个特定大小的新空表。

然后 PATCH 消息将该空表转换为发送者的当前状态。

未更改的条目表示为零或其他无操作值。 因为大多数条目通常不会改变，所以那些长串的零在网络上压缩得非常好。

RESET和PATCH消息是直连消息。 它们不会在 Gnutella 网络中泛滥。

补丁序列可以分为多个消息，但接收者期望完整的序列能够解释逻辑表中的每个槽。

将查询与表匹配

当搜索到达时，接收客户端执行与构建表时使用的大致相同的规范化过程。

查询例如：

Ubuntu Server ISO

成为 QRP 索引词的向量。

每个索引项都被散列到表槽中。 客户端检查这些槽是否存在于与连接关联的表中。

显而易见的算法要求每个单词都出现：

ubuntu = present
server = present
iso    = present

即使缺少一个单词，查询也不会通过该连接发送。 大多数客户端应用模糊匹配，在满足特定阈值时发送查询。

额外功能：超到超查询

我提到过 QRP 表用于 leaf-to-ultrapeer 通信。 有时QRP表可用于超到超查询路由。

超级节点可以从其自己的共享文件和合格的直接连接的叶子提供的 QRP 表构建组合表。

超级对等体可以将该组合表发送到通告对最后一跳QRP的支持的相邻超级对等体。

组合表表明超级对等点自己的文件或其直接连接的叶子之一可能与这些术语匹配。 重要的是要记住，这不会尝试将资源映射到超级对等直接连接的叶子之外。

假设一个超级对等体正在考虑向相邻的超级对等体发送查询。

如果查询剩余多于一跳，ultrapeer 不会使用邻居的 QRP 表作为硬过滤器。 邻居可能会将查询进一步转发到网络中，超出其本地表所表示的文件。

如果查询仅剩一跳，情况就不同了。

相邻的超级对等点无法通过主干网将查询发送得更远。 它所能做的就是检查它自己的文件并将查询转发到它的叶子。

这些正是其组合的 QRP 表所代表的资源。

此时，负的 QRP 匹配就变得有用了，因为可以确认最后一跳上没有任何可用的内容可以匹配，因此不需要在那里发送查询。

结论

QRP 是晚期 Gnutella 客户端的一个有趣但被忽视的方面，它幸存到了今天的网络中。 它能够有效地大规模过滤噪声，而无需分布式哈希表所需的精确性。 我不知道除了 Gnutella2 之外还有任何现有的 P2P 系统使用 QRP 表进行内容发现。

本文未涉及

有几个实施细节值得自己研究：

  • 四位和一位 PATCH 协商
  • 补丁压缩
  • Unicode 规范化
  • SHA-1 和其他 URN 搜索
  • 处理变得过于密集的表
  • 啤酒花流量限制
  • 过载叶子的流量控制行为
  • 表重建和传输的频率
  • 客户端之间的兼容性差异
  • GUESS，提供了另一种执行有针对性的 Gnutella 搜索的方法
